steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The conclusion rests on one fundamental assumption: spontaneous and volitional laughter are acoustically distinct enough that listeners can reliably tell them apart.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice A states that volitional laughter “often shares certain acoustic qualities” with spontaneous laughter in real-world contexts. If the two types share acoustic properties, the discrimination task is harder, directly undermining the claim that a universal ability to distinguish them is demonstrated. B, C, and D either identify distinguishing features or confirm methodological details; none undermines the core assumption. That matches choice A — A is correct.
